findings.title: persistExtractionResults is 68 lines with 4 sequential blocks
findings.description: This method handles persistence for 4 different entity types (memories, profileFacts, relationships, importantDates) in a single function spanning 68 lines. While each block is straightforward, the overall function exceeds the 50-line threshold and could be decomposed into focused helper methods for readability. Additionally, the memories block generates two different UUIDs for the same memory (line 125 for VectorStore, line 141 for DocumentStore), making cross-store correlation impossible.

findings.title: cosineSimilarity imported from smart-todo is a shared utility
findings.description: cosineSimilarity is a pure math utility function that is now used by both smart-todo.ts and memory-extractor.ts. Importing it from a domain-specific module (smart-todo) creates a misleading dependency -- memory-extractor appears to depend on smart-todo when it only needs a math helper. This cross-domain import will confuse dependency analysis and make future refactoring harder.

findings.title: Magic number 3 in semanticSearch candidate multiplier
findings.code_snippet: const candidates = await this.search(tableName, queryVector, topK * 3)
findings.description: The multiplier 3 in `topK * 3` is a magic number. It represents the over-fetch ratio to compensate for threshold filtering but is not documented as a named constant. This makes it harder to tune or understand the tradeoff.

findings.title: Redundant double cast in importantDates validation
findings.description: After `isValidImportantDate` has already validated that d has `date`, `dateType`, and `label` as strings, the map function casts d through `unknown` back to Record and then casts each field again. The type guard already narrows d to ExtractedImportantDate, making most of this redundant. The only added value is the `description` fallback, which could be done more directly.

positive_observations: All functions in the changed files are short and focused, with clear single responsibilities (except persistExtractionResults noted above)
Consistent use of early returns and guard clauses throughout (e.g., dedup empty checks, VectorStore.search empty check)
Validation functions use proper type guard pattern (value is Type) with clean null/type checks
DocumentStore methods follow a consistent, readable prepare/run pattern with proper parameterized queries (no SQL injection)
The MemoryExtractor class cleanly separates extraction, validation, and dedup into distinct methods
VectorStore uses a static factory method (create) to handle async initialization, avoiding constructor async antipattern
